assertions.title: Launch focus and Tab loop stay predictable

steps.action: Launch the seeded-company TUI session
steps.expected: Composer is initially focused
steps.observed: Startup frame showed `focus: composer` with the message input active.

steps.action: Press `Tab` once
steps.expected: Primary focus moves to management
steps.observed: Status bar changed to `focus: management`.

steps.action: Press `Tab` again
steps.expected: Primary focus returns to the composer
steps.observed: Status bar returned to `focus: composer`.

steps.action: From management, open Settings and press `Tab` while the overlay is visible
steps.expected: Overlay captures focus and the background Tab loop is suspended
steps.observed: Both overlay snapshots stayed on `focus: overlay`; pressing `Tab` did not move focus underneath the overlay.

assertions.title: Message submit trims content, ignores empty sends, and clears after success

steps.action: Press `Enter` with an empty composer
steps.expected: No send occurs and no new transcript entry appears
steps.observed: The chat remained `No messages yet` and no waiting state appeared.

steps.action: Type whitespace only and press `Enter`
steps.expected: No send occurs and no new transcript entry appears
steps.observed: The chat still showed `No messages yet`; the composer kept only the visible spaces until manually cleared.

steps.action: Type `  say ok only  ` and press `Enter`
steps.expected: One trimmed user turn is appended, the composer enters a waiting state, and the composer clears after the successful response
steps.observed: The transcript appended `You: say ok only`, the UI showed `Waiting for response...`, and after the `Orchestrator: ok` reply the composer returned to `Type a message...`.

keypressTranscript: Space, Space, s, a, y, Space, o, k, Space, o, n, l, y, Space, Space, Enter

assertions.title: Waiting and failure states return to a retryable composer

steps.action: Launch the seeded-company TUI and enter `run command sleep 15 and then echo ok` with explicit per-key input.
steps.expected: A live send can be started without duplicating prior user turns.
steps.observed: One user turn was appended and the composer stayed on the original session context.

steps.action: Press `Enter` and capture the live waiting state.
steps.expected: The composer visibly enters the waiting state before the turn completes.
steps.observed: The transcript showed `You: run command sleep 15 and then echo ok` and the footer showed `Waiting for response...` with `Codex: thinking`.

steps.action: Identify the TUI-owned Codex subprocess and terminate it after waiting began.
steps.expected: The live Codex-side failure clears the waiting state, surfaces an error, and returns the composer to an immediate retry path.
steps.observed: Using `ps -axo pid,ppid,command | grep -E 'scripts/dev-tui\.mjs|packages/orchestrator-tui|codex app-server' | grep -v grep` identified TUI process `13484` with child `13493`; `kill 13493` terminated that subprocess and the TUI auto-spawned replacement PID `13827`, but the turn never surfaced an error or cleared the waiting state.

steps.action: Press `r` after the Codex subprocess restart and observe the same session.
steps.expected: The operator can immediately type/retry from the same session without a duplicate user turn.
steps.observed: No visible retry draft appeared, no duplicate user turn was appended, and the shell remained stuck on `Waiting for response...` more than 15 seconds after the kill.

keypressTranscript: per-key input: run command sleep 15 and then echo ok

inspect: ps -axo pid,ppid,command | grep -E 'scripts/dev-tui\.mjs|packages/orchestrator-tui|codex app-server' | grep -v grep

assertions.issues: FAIL: after inducing a real live-send disruption by killing the TUI-owned `codex app-server` subprocess during the waiting state, the TUI auto-reconnected Codex but never cleared `Waiting for response...`, never showed a failure message, and did not become visibly retryable from the same session.

assertions.title: Typing in the composer suppresses global shortcut side effects

steps.action: Focus the composer and press printable shortcut letters `s`, `c`, `n`
steps.expected: The keys are inserted into the draft instead of opening settings, company switching, or the issue composer
steps.observed: The draft became `scn`, no overlay appeared, and the status bar stayed on `focus: composer`.

assertions.title: Opening and dismissing company switching without selecting a new company is a no-op

steps.action: With the existing transcript visible and an empty composer, open the company switcher from management
steps.expected: The switcher opens over the existing company session
steps.observed: `Switch Company` opened with `focus: overlay` while the existing `You: say ok only` transcript remained the session context behind it.

steps.action: Dismiss the switcher with `Escape` without selecting a company
steps.expected: Active company, transcript, draft state, focus target, and selection context are preserved
steps.observed: After dismissal the session was still on `Seeded Focus Co`, the transcript still showed the earlier exchange, the composer remained empty (`Type a message...`), and focus returned to `management`.

assertions.title: Visible draft state stays synchronized with shortcut gating across overlay lifecycles

steps.action: Create a visible draft (`scn`), move to management, open and dismiss Settings, then press `c`
steps.expected: The visible draft remains the gating source of truth, so draft-sensitive shortcuts stay blocked after the overlay closes
steps.observed: The draft stayed visible as `> scn` before and after the Settings overlay, and pressing `c` afterward did not open company switching.

assertions.title: Exactly one interactive region is visibly active at a time

steps.action: Observe startup, Tab to management, and open the company switcher overlay
steps.expected: Composer, management, and overlay each advertise themselves as the sole active region when focused
steps.observed: The status bar showed `focus: composer` at launch, `focus: management` after `Tab`, and `focus: overlay` when `Switch Company` opened.

steps.action: With the overlay open, press `j`
steps.expected: Only the overlay remains active; adjacent regions stay inert
steps.observed: The overlay stayed open and the background session context did not mutate underneath it.

assertions.title: Modal overlays capture input and restore prior context on dismiss

steps.action: Open the company switcher from management
steps.expected: The modal overlay captures input and becomes the active surface
steps.observed: The `Switch Company` overlay opened and the status bar changed to `focus: overlay`.

steps.action: Press `j` while the switcher is open
steps.expected: Background management shortcuts do not fire under the overlay
steps.observed: The switcher remained unchanged and no background issue navigation or transcript mutation occurred.

steps.action: Dismiss the overlay with `Escape`
steps.expected: The prior management context is restored
steps.observed: Dismissal returned to the same `Seeded Focus Co` management session with the earlier transcript intact and focus back on management.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: 7 passed and 1 failed. VAL-FOCUS-003 was re-run with a deterministic live Codex subprocess kill after the waiting state began; the TUI auto-reconnected Codex but left the composer stuck in `Waiting for response...` without a visible error or retry path.
